title: Calvaria inermis
taxonomy: Calvaria inermis is classified within the family Apocynaceae, which is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This classification is based on the plant's morphology and genetic characteristics. Calvaria inermis is further categorized as a species within the genus Calvaria, which is native to tropical and subtropical regions. The Apocynaceae family is a large and diverse group of plants, including many species of vines, shrubs, and trees. intro: Calvaria inermis, commonly known as the yellow guinea pepper or yellow Guinea pepper tree, is a plant species native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its ability to aid in wound healing and alleviate symptoms of respiratory infections.
